page: Amazon Web Services m8gn.xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an arm64 architecture utilizing an AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate average performance across most standard operations. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests, along with Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores, fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth is measured at 462.06 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 96.33 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. While single-threaded bzip3 decompression shows strong performance in the top 25 percent tier, the instance exhibits notable weaknesses in other areas. Specifically, Passmark CPU encryption tests register in the bottom 10 percent tier, and Passmark CPU extended instructions score in the bottom 25 percent. Large language model inference speeds are also limited, with text generation for a 7B model reaching only 6.53 tokens per second.

From a resource density perspective, the m8gn.xlarge offers a balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 1 to 4, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation without the overhead of hyperthreading. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires users to factor in the latency of EBS volumes. The instance is suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, database operations, and software compilation, but is less suitable for cryptographic-heavy tasks or local machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by an arm64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, 16.0 GB of system memory, and a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, with no local storage or GPU hardware. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier for most general compute, memory bandwidth, and web serving tasks, though it demonstrates strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Conversely, it exhibits weak performance in cryptographic encryption and extended CPU instructions, as well as limited capabilities for local large language model inference. The instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, and database operations that require dedicated CPU allocation but do not demand high-performance local storage or intensive cryptographic processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 16 GB RAM, 12 Gbps network bandwidth, and average overall benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.xlarge is a general-purpose arm64 instance with 4 dedicated Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 general-purpose instance with dedicated Graviton cores and balanced memory, optimized for network-reliant workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: Powered by an arm64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores.
Equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory and a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Delivers average performance in general compute, database operations, and static web serving.
Demonstrates strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression performance.
Lacks local NVMe storage and shows weak performance in cryptographic encryption tasks.
Suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, and software compilation.
